$comment: Normatively defined in AIP spec §5.1. The registration envelope is the outermost container for POST /v1/agents. It is NOT signed as a whole — each sub-document (capability_manifest, principal_token) carries its own signature. See AIP spec §5.2 for the full ordered validation algorithm (Checks 1–14).
title: AIP Registration Envelope
description: The request body submitted to POST /v1/agents to register a new AIP agent. Defined in AIP spec §5.1. Contains three top-level fields: identity (the Core Identity Object), capability_manifest (the initial Capability Manifest), and principal_token (a compact JWT encoding the root delegation from the principal to this agent). The Registry validates all three before persisting the agent.

identity.description: The agent's Core Identity Object. MUST conform to agent-identity.schema.json. The aid field in identity MUST match the aid field in capability_manifest. The public_key.kid MUST be anchored to the same aid.

capability_manifest.description: The agent's initial Capability Manifest. MUST conform to capability-manifest.schema.json. The aid field MUST match identity.aid. The version MUST be 1 for initial registration. The signature MUST be verifiable using the granted_by DID's public key.

principal_token.description: A compact-serialised signed JWT (header.payload.signature) encoding the root delegation Principal Token that authorises this agent. The JWT payload MUST conform to principal-token.schema.json with delegation_depth=0 and delegated_by=null. The JWT header MUST have typ='JWT' and alg='EdDSA'. The sub field MUST equal identity.aid. The JWT MUST be signed by the root principal identified in principal.id. Validated by AIP spec §5.2 Registration Checks 8–11.

grant_tier.description: The AIP-GRANT tier used to obtain the principal_token. REQUIRED. G1 = Registry-Mediated Grant; G2 = Direct Deployer grant; G3 = OAuth 2.1 Full Ceremony. MUST be consistent with scope tiers: G1 is permitted only when all capability_manifest scopes are Tier 1; G2 or G3 are required when any scope is Tier 2; G3 is required when any scope is Tier 3. Validated by AIP spec §5.2 Check 14.

registration_checks.$comment: AIP spec §5.2 Registration Checks (validated by the Registry in order): 1) identity MUST be present and valid JSON. 2) identity.aid MUST match did:aip ABNF. 3) identity.type MUST match namespace of identity.aid. 4) identity.aid MUST NOT already be registered (CodeAlreadyRegistered). 5) identity.public_key MUST be OKP/Ed25519 with 43-char x. 6) capability_manifest.expires_at MUST be in the future. 7) capability_manifest.aid MUST equal identity.aid. 8) principal_token MUST be a valid 3-part compact JWT. 9) principal_token header MUST have typ=JWT and alg=EdDSA. 10) principal_token payload sub MUST equal identity.aid. 11) For ephemeral agents, principal_token payload task_id MUST be non-null and non-empty. 12) capability_manifest.signature MUST verify using the granted_by public key. 13) If identity.version >= 2, identity.previous_key_signature MUST verify using the previous key. 14) grant_tier MUST be present, MUST be one of G1/G2/G3, and MUST be consistent with the scope tiers of the capability_manifest.
